stream_samples.text: uber quoted me $14, charged $32 after a 4-min wait. surge timer never appeared on the price screen, just on the receipt
ETA in app said 3 min, driver was 11 min away and stuck behind a parade. Routing engine clearly didn't pull the SF closure feed.
[#RIDE-44218 P1] rider tipped $4.00, driver received $3.97. Rounding gap repro'd across 18 trips in NYC last week.
Got dispatched to a pickup 2.4 mi away when 6 drivers were idle within 0.5 mi. Pairing model is way off in Midtown after 6pm.
Uber One auto-renewed at $9.99 even though I cancelled in March. Took 4 chats to get a refund. iOS 18.2.
navigation in the driver app sent me down 14th Street which has been closed for construction since February. Google Maps knew.
Pairing latency p95 is up 480ms in dense urban tiles since the v3.4 dispatcher rollout. ETA accuracy regressed 9% in NYC/SF.
Surge multiplier showed 1.0x on the map, then 1.8x on the confirm screen. Felt like a bait-and-switch even if it's a race condition.
[#RIDE-44551] driver canceled after accepting, rider charged a $5 cancellation fee. This is the third report this morning.
Tip from a 5-star rider showed in earnings, then disappeared 20 min later. Support said "adjustment in progress" with no detail.
the new live activity for arriving drivers on iOS is genuinely great, finally don't have to keep the app open the whole time
[dispatcher-v3] supply-side cost function over-weights idle time, causing far-driver pairings in dense tiles. needs hotfix.
anyone else notice the ETA always rounds DOWN now? feels like a UX choice but the variance is wild during rush hour
Routing took rider through a closed road in Boston, driver had to U-turn, rider charged extra time. Refunded but the root cause keeps recurring.
Driver showed up 7 min after the quoted ETA. App said "arriving now" the whole time. Android 14, Pixel 8.

clusters.title: Driver-rider pairing regression in dense urban tiles
ETA accuracy degraded post dispatcher v3.4
Surge multiplier mismatch between map and confirm
Tip flow rounding and adjustment gaps
Navigation routes through known closed roads
Uber One cancel-then-renewal billing edge cases

top_epic.title: Fix driver-rider pairing regression in dense urban tiles
top_epic.rationale: 2,380 cross-channel signals tie to the v3.4 dispatcher rollout: drivers report being pulled past closer idle peers, riders see ETA accuracy regress 9% in NYC/SF, and Jira flags an over-weighted idle-time term in the supply-side cost function. Pairing quality is the load-bearing primitive — when it slips, every downstream metric (ETA, cancellations, surge perception) slips with it.
top_epic.verdict: Marketplace-critical — pairing quality compounds across every trip.
top_epic.recommendation: Roll back the idle-time weight in dense tiles, ship a hotfix to the cost function, and gate future dispatcher changes behind tile-level shadow evals.
acceptance_criteria: Reweight idle-time term in dispatcher cost function for tiles with supply density > 4 drivers/km^2
Pairing distance p95 returns to baseline (under 0.9 mi) in NYC/SF/LA dense tiles within 7 days
ETA accuracy regression closes to within 2% of pre-v3.4 baseline across the top 25 metros
Shadow-eval gate added to dispatcher CI: any tile-level pairing-distance regression > 5% blocks merge
